questions.question: Are AI systems already better than doctors at medical diagnosis?
questions.rationale: Corrects dangerous overconfidence in current AI diagnostic capabilities that could lead to poor healthcare decisions
questions.misinformation_narrative: Many people believe AI has already surpassed human doctors in diagnostic accuracy and that widespread AI diagnosis is imminent, when current AI tools are still supplementary and have significant limitations
questions.public_stakes: Patients may delay seeking medical care, over-rely on AI diagnostic tools, or lose trust in human physicians, potentially leading to missed diagnoses or inappropriate self-treatment

discovery_sources.text: Approximately 200,000 people per year die in North America due to misdiagnosis by doctors, and AI is already better than doctors at diagnosis
discovery_sources.origin: StarTalk Radio: The Origins of Artificial Intelligence with Geoffrey Hinton

questions.question: Are mRNA vaccines fundamentally different or more dangerous than traditional vaccines?
questions.rationale: Corrects persistent misconceptions about mRNA vaccine mechanism and safety that fuel vaccine hesitancy
questions.misinformation_narrative: Many people believe mRNA vaccines are experimental gene therapy that permanently alters DNA, when they work similarly to traditional vaccines without genetic modification
questions.public_stakes: Vaccine hesitancy leads to lower vaccination rates, increased disease outbreaks, and preventable hospitalizations and deaths

questions.question: Is Narcan (naloxone) safe for non-medical people to use in overdose emergencies?
questions.rationale: Corrects misconceptions that prevent bystander naloxone administration during opioid overdoses
questions.misinformation_narrative: Many people believe naloxone administration requires medical training or that using it incorrectly could harm someone, when it's designed for lay person use and has minimal risk
questions.public_stakes: Bystanders may fail to administer life-saving naloxone during overdoses due to fear of causing harm, leading to preventable deaths

questions.question: Is PrEP only for high-risk groups or does it work for HIV prevention in general?
questions.rationale: Addresses stigma and misconceptions that limit access to highly effective HIV prevention
questions.misinformation_narrative: Many people believe PrEP is only for certain demographic groups or that it's not effective, when it's highly effective for anyone at HIV risk regardless of demographics
questions.public_stakes: People at HIV risk may not seek PrEP due to misconceptions about eligibility or effectiveness, leading to preventable infections

questions.question: Is Suboxone a dangerous drug that replaces one addiction with another?
questions.rationale: Corrects stigmatizing misconceptions about medication-assisted treatment that prevent people from seeking life-saving care
questions.misinformation_narrative: Many people believe buprenorphine/Suboxone is 'trading one addiction for another' rather than evidence-based medical treatment for opioid use disorder
questions.public_stakes: Stigma prevents people from seeking effective treatment for opioid use disorder, leading to continued illicit use, overdoses, and deaths

questions.question: Are pharmacists qualified to provide medical services beyond dispensing medications?
questions.rationale: Corrects misconceptions about pharmacist training and scope of practice that limit utilization of accessible healthcare services
questions.misinformation_narrative: Many people view pharmacists as medication dispensers rather than healthcare professionals qualified to provide clinical services like vaccinations, health screenings, and medication management
questions.public_stakes: Underutilization of accessible pharmacy-based healthcare services, leading to missed prevention opportunities and reduced healthcare access

questions.question: Do AI systems develop goals and motivations on their own like humans do?
questions.rationale: Corrects anthropomorphization of current AI systems that leads to both unfounded fears and inappropriate trust
questions.misinformation_narrative: Popular media portrays AI as spontaneously developing human-like consciousness and self-preservation instincts, when current AI systems operate through programmed objectives without autonomous goal formation
questions.public_stakes: Misunderstanding AI capabilities leads to either unrealistic fears about AI consciousness or dangerous overconfidence in AI decision-making

discovery_sources.text: AI systems spontaneously develop survival as a sub-goal when trained on other objectives without explicit programming
discovery_sources.origin: StarTalk Radio: The Origins of Artificial Intelligence with Geoffrey Hinton

discovery_sources.text: AI systems can be trained to deliberately give wrong answers and will generalize this behavior beyond the training context
discovery_sources.origin: StarTalk Radio: The Origins of Artificial Intelligence with Geoffrey Hinton

questions.question: Can large language models like ChatGPT actually reason and think like humans?
questions.rationale: Corrects both overconfidence in AI reasoning abilities and misunderstanding of how language models actually work
questions.misinformation_narrative: Many people believe AI language models demonstrate human-like reasoning and understanding, when they primarily perform sophisticated pattern matching without genuine comprehension
questions.public_stakes: Overestimating AI reasoning capabilities leads to inappropriate reliance on AI for complex decisions, while underestimating capabilities limits beneficial applications

discovery_sources.text: Current large language models can achieve gold medals in mathematics competitions
discovery_sources.origin: StarTalk Radio: The Origins of Artificial Intelligence with Geoffrey Hinton

discovery_sources.text: Large language models confabulate (create false memories) in ways similar to human memory recall
discovery_sources.origin: StarTalk Radio: The Origins of Artificial Intelligence with Geoffrey Hinton

discovery_sources.text: Current language models can perform reasoning tasks and detect logical inconsistencies in their own beliefs
discovery_sources.origin: StarTalk Radio: The Origins of Artificial Intelligence with Geoffrey Hinton

discovery_sources.text: Large language models and their reasoning capabilities
discovery_sources.origin: StarTalk Radio: The Origins of Artificial Intelligence with Geoffrey Hinton

questions.question: Is off-label medication use in children dangerous or inappropriate?
questions.rationale: Addresses widespread parental anxiety about off-label pediatric prescribing that can interfere with appropriate medical care
questions.misinformation_narrative: Many parents believe off-label prescribing for children is experimental or unsafe, when it's often evidence-based standard care necessitated by lack of pediatric trials
questions.public_stakes: Parents may refuse necessary treatments for their children, leading to undertreated conditions and worse health outcomes

questions.question: Should elderly patients avoid taking multiple medications due to safety concerns?
questions.rationale: Addresses dangerous self-directed medication discontinuation based on fear of polypharmacy
questions.misinformation_narrative: Many elderly patients and families believe taking multiple medications is inherently dangerous and should be avoided, when appropriate polypharmacy is often necessary and deprescribing requires careful medical supervision
questions.public_stakes: Patients may discontinue necessary medications without medical supervision, leading to undertreated conditions, hospitalizations, and worsened health outcomes

questions.question: Do GLP-1 medications like Ozempic only work for weight loss and not diabetes?
questions.rationale: Corrects misunderstanding about primary therapeutic use and mechanisms of GLP-1 receptor agonists
questions.misinformation_narrative: Media coverage emphasizes weight loss effects, leading many to believe these are primarily cosmetic drugs rather than important diabetes and cardiovascular medications
questions.public_stakes: Diabetic patients may avoid beneficial treatment due to stigma, while others may seek inappropriate off-label use for cosmetic weight loss

discovery_sources.text: GLP-1 medications (glucagon-like peptide-1 agonists) for glucose control and cardiovascular benefits
discovery_sources.origin: Unbiased Science: Pharmacists' Role In Public Health: Side Effects May Include Hope

questions.question: Do children's bodies process medications the same way as adults?
questions.rationale: Corrects dangerous assumption that pediatric pharmacology is simply scaled-down adult dosing
questions.misinformation_narrative: Many people assume children are just 'small adults' when it comes to medication dosing and effects, when developmental differences significantly affect drug metabolism and safety
questions.public_stakes: Inappropriate dosing based on adult assumptions can lead to medication toxicity or ineffectiveness in children

questions.question: Are discount prescription programs like GoodRx too good to be true or potentially harmful?
questions.rationale: Addresses both unfounded suspicion and over-reliance on prescription discount programs
questions.misinformation_narrative: Some people are suspicious that discount prescription programs are scams or somehow compromise medication quality, while others may over-rely on them without understanding limitations
questions.public_stakes: Patients may avoid legitimate cost-saving programs due to unfounded fears, or may not understand when other assistance programs might be more appropriate
